hypothesis: Introducing bulky aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in ECL2/TM5 while reducing off-target affinity to SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs show strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient structural diversity and sampling. Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions for selectivity while increasing candidate numbers should improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pass rate suggests insufficient sampling of the design space. Increasing the number of candidates may yield more structurally valid designs.
To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT at 7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
